name: Gotify
description: OpenClaw Gotify channel plugin — REST delivery, WebSocket stream inbound, multi-account session isolation, and full application/client lifecycle management.

gotify.label: Gotify
gotify.description: Self-hosted push notification server — send and receive messages via REST API and WebSocket stream

appToken.description: Application token for sending messages (prefix A...)

clientToken.description: Client token for receiving messages via WebSocket stream and admin APIs (prefix C...)

dmPolicy.description: DM access policy for inbound stream messages (matched by appid or peerId)

allowFrom.description: Allowed appid/peerId entries when dmPolicy is allowlist (use "*" for open policy hint)

enabled.description: Enable WebSocket stream listener for real-time message receiving

deleteAfterConsume.description: Delete Gotify message after inbound is dispatched (set false for dev/test visibility)

accounts.description: Multiple Gotify account configurations for multi-account multi-agent routing

defaultAccount.description: Default account ID when using multi-account mode

serverUrl.label: Gotify Server URL
serverUrl.help: The base URL of your Gotify server (e.g. https://gotify.example.com)

appToken.label: App Token
appToken.help: Application token for sending messages. Created in Gotify WebUI under Apps. Prefix: A...

clientToken.label: Client Token
clientToken.help: Client token for receiving messages via WebSocket stream and managing applications/clients. Created in Gotify WebUI under Clients. Prefix: C...

defaultPriority.label: Default Priority
defaultPriority.help: Message priority level (0-10, default 5). Higher values indicate higher importance.

inbound.enabled.label: Enable WebSocket Stream
inbound.enabled.help: Connect to Gotify /stream endpoint for real-time message receiving. Requires clientToken.

inbound.deleteAfterConsume.label: Delete After Consume
inbound.deleteAfterConsume.help: Remove message from Gotify after the agent receives it. Set false to keep messages for debugging or OPENCLAW_TEST_VISIBLE-style workflows.

dmPolicy.label: DM Policy
dmPolicy.help: Control who can trigger the agent via inbound stream (open, allowlist, pairing, disabled).

allowFrom.label: Allow From
allowFrom.help: Allowed appid or peerId values when dmPolicy is allowlist. Use ["*"] with dmPolicy open as a documented wildcard.

bootstrap.enabled.label: Bootstrap Enabled
bootstrap.enabled.help: Run bootstrap helpers (auto-create Application) when configured.
